rulings.Case Title: ZERONG MA ET AL VS. FILLMORE CENTER ASSOCIATES LP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production And An Award Of Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 12, 2021, Line 1, DEFENDANT GREYSTAR REAL ESTATE PARTNERS, LLC's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production And An Award Of Sanctions Against Plaintiffs' Counsel. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The unopposed Motion is granted. Plaintiffs shall provide verified responses, without objections, within ten calendar days. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiffs in the amount of $746. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 12, 2021, Line 4, DEBRA SCHOENBERG's Motion To Quash Subpoena For Personal Appearance [Code Civ. Proc. Secs. 1985.3, 1987.1, 1987.2]. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to quash is granted. Plaintiff has not demonstrated "extremely good cause" to compel Ms. Schoenberg's deposition. No sanctions. rulings.Case Title: RYAN EMISON ET AL VS. EDMUND OZIFO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents; Declaration Of Anthony Werbin, Esq.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 12, 2021, Line 9, PLAINTIFFS RYAN EMISON AND NADIN CUTTER's Motion For An Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted. There is no time limit by which to bring a motion for an order deeming facts admitted. Here, the parties signed a stipulation that states, in part: [A]ny dates that have already passed, including . . . motion . . . deadlines will remained closed . . . ." There was never a motion deadline for this Motion. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: RANSOM LAB INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. TECHIFY SOLUTIONS PVT. LTD. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 12, 2021, Line 7, DEFENDANTS SHAIL TALATI, TECHIFY SOLUTIONS PVT. LTD. DBA X2SKY's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT: Defendants' demurrer to the first amended complaint's (FAC) third cause of action for negligent misrepresentation is sustained. This new cause of action - not authorized by my prior demurrer order - requires a motion for leave to amend. (CCP 472.) The demurrer to the FAC's fourth cause of action for fraud is sustained with leave to amend. The FAC still does not plead with the required specificity - "how, when, where, to whom, and by what means." (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS ADJUSTMENT BUREAU, INC. VS. TOUCHOFMODERN, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Touch Of Moderns Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default, Memorandum Of Points And Authories With Exhibit A
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 12, 2021, Line 8, DEFENDANT TOUCHOFMODERN, INC's Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default: Defendant Touch of Modern's motion to set aside entry of default is granted. (CCP 473.) The motion was filed less than three months after entry of default and our supreme court directs that "[b]ecause the law favors disposing of cases on their merits, any doubts in applying section 473 must be resolved in favor of the party seeking relief from default." (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 980.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MICHELE WALKER VS. CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant City And County Of San Francisco To Requests For Admission, Set One, Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And To Compel Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 12, 2021, Line 5, PLAINTIFF MICHELE WALKER's Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant City And County Of San Francisco To Requests For Admission, Set One, Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And To Compel Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: Requests for Admission 1, 2, 3, 4, 8 and 11 are granted. CCSF must answer the Requests as written. If confused by the actual text of the Requests, then the appropriate response is to deny and then explain the denial in Form Interrogatory 17.1. Where applicable, CCSF must also aver that it has undertaken a reasonable inquiry. Requests for Admission 14 and 15 are denied. The term "schedule" in this context is vague. Form Interrogatories 12.4 and 12.5 are granted. Requests for Production 1, 2, 5, 8, 10, 11, 12, 16, 19, 20, and 21 are granted subject to a mutually agreed to protective order if the parties so desire. Requests for Production 6 and 7 are denied. Among other reasons, the term "complaint" is overbroad (e.g. a complaint for sexual harassment would be irrelevant). Request 14 is denied as irrelevant to any pled cause of action. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IDEMITSU RENEWABLES AMERICA INC. VS. GCL SYSTEM INTEGRATION TECHNOLOGY LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 12, 2021, Line 10, DEFENDANTS GCL SYSTEM INTEGRATION TECHNOLOGY LLC, ZHANG JIAGANG GCL SYSTEM INTEGRATION TECHNOLOGY's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT: Defendants' demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled. Defendants' sole argument is that "standing and misjoinder" bar both plaintiffs because "only one of" them "can have a claim against Defendants." However, California law liberally permits pleading in the alternative. (See, e.g., CCP 378.) The facts and law as to which plaintiff (or both) holds the claims can be developed in the litigation; this is merely the pleading stage. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: SALLY L. LIU VS. BRUCE R. BEASLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Sally L. Liu To Attend Deposition Request For Sanctions; Declaration Of Tom Normandin In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 12, 2021, Line 6, 2- Defendant Brian Getz's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Sally L. Liu To Attend Deposition Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)
